catalog.rationale: Designed around Fujifilm's natural-photo compact-camera system, it made flashless ambient snapshots its central aesthetic.

reference.relationship: Independent interpretive emulation; researched, not measured

dossier.tagline: Soft low-light color, cool-green shadows, and expressive high-speed grain.
dossier.portrait: NATURA 1600 is modeled around soft low-light color, cool-green shadows, and expressive high-speed grain. Designed around Fujifilm's natural-photo compact-camera system, it made flashless ambient snapshots its central aesthetic. Grainlab treats that identity as a linked capture, emulsion, process, and output system—not as a color cast pasted over a digital file.

facts.label: CANON RANK
facts.note: Curatorial significance rank; not a sales chart

chapters.title: Why NATURA 1600 belongs in the century canon
chapters.lede: Designed around Fujifilm's natural-photo compact-camera system, it made flashless ambient snapshots its central aesthetic.

details.label: Material
details.value: C-41 color negative; daylight; nominal speed / EI reference 1600.

details.label: Image structure
details.value: tabular crystal interpretation, mixed emulsion mapping, coarse presentation-scale grain.

notes: The canon balances cultural use, technical influence, geographic/manufacturer breadth, process diversity, and surviving documentation; rank is curatorial rather than a claimed global sales measurement.

chapters.title: Tone, color, and material signature
chapters.lede: The target is soft low-light color, cool-green shadows, and expressive high-speed grain.

details.label: Curve
details.value: Toe 0.17 · development gamma 1.04 · shoulder 0.34 · scan contrast 1.00.

details.label: Color / spectral posture
details.value: greenPastel channel sensitivity and density-dependent crossover; daylight reference balance.

details.label: Grain
details.value: coarse correlated dye-cloud density variation; tabular geometry; mixed architecture.

details.label: Halation
details.value: 5% thresholded scene-highlight scatter before the film curve; intentionally stock/process dependent.

notes: Grain is signal-dependent and spatially correlated across the full frame; it is not independent RGB noise or a repeating overlay.

chapters.title: From DSLR or iPhone to a plausible negative
chapters.lede: Begin from a neutral SDR rendering. Disable or minimize phone HDR, vivid styles, skin smoothing, and sharpening; preserve channel detail before the negative curve and dye-layer crossover are applied.

details.label: Input normalization
details.value: Prefer RAW developed neutrally when available. For HEIC/JPEG, choose the least processed rendition; avoid baked Vivid styles, portrait relighting, clarity, and denoise.

details.label: Dynamic range
details.value: Map HDR/Display-P3 into a stable SDR working image without clipping, preserving highlight and shadow separation before the film response.

details.label: White balance
details.value: Neutralize obvious camera casts first, then let the daylight stock balance and emulsion sensitivity create the intended response.

details.label: Hard limit
details.value: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.

notes: Apple ProRAW and camera RAW still need a deliberate neutral development; RAW is not automatically scene-linear or aesthetically neutral after a converter renders it.

chapters.title: A stage-by-stage rendering target
chapters.lede: sRGB decode → +0.04 EV scene placement → 0.980/1.035/1.000 channel sensitivity → toe 0.17, γ 1.04, shoulder 0.34 → C-41 color negative chemistry → 5% thresholded halation → coarse dye-cloud structure

details.label: Scene
details.value: Decode to linear light, apply +0.04 EV and the stock sensitivity before tone shaping.

details.label: Emulsion
details.value: Build the toe, midtone gamma, shoulder, crossover, saturation compression, and correlated image structure as one response.

details.label: Lab / optics
details.value: Model C-41 color negative, chemical fog, local density contrast, and highlight scatter before the output transform.

details.label: Scan / print
details.value: Use a neutral scan baseline, set black and white points after the film curve, and sharpen subject detail at final output size.

notes: All parameters are bounded artistic approximations derived from published characteristics and process knowledge, not digitized manufacturer sensitometric or spectral measurements.

bestFor: Subjects that benefit from soft low-light color, cool-green shadows, and expressive high-speed grain.
Scenes whose palette and contrast align with the stock rather than fighting it.
watchFor: A rendered DSLR/iPhone file already contains sensor spectral response, computational tone mapping, demosaic, white balance, and gamut decisions; this profile cannot reverse information those stages discarded.
Stacking a strong camera picture style, computational HDR, or sharpening underneath the emulation.
Judging grain at 200% instead of at 100% and intended output size.
fieldNotes: Expose for the material idea: soft low-light color, cool-green shadows, and expressive high-speed grain.
Try the profile on neutral daylight, mixed light, skin, foliage, and a flat wall before deciding what its name means.

sources.title: Negative and reversal film data-sheet archive

dossier.disclaimer: NATURA 1600 is an independent Grainlab artistic emulation informed by cited public literature and historical material. It is not endorsed by Fujifilm, is not a measured ICC/LUT profile, and cannot reproduce a specific batch, lens, exposure, chemistry, printer, scanner, paper, aging state, or wavelengths absent from the digital source.
